info.description: Personal AI agent management API — multi-agent routing, LLM providers, tools, approvals, and more.
info.title: Denkeeper API

get.description: Returns all registered agents with metadata

post.description: Creates a new agent at runtime with persona directory and TOML persistence

get.description: Returns detailed configuration for a single agent

delete.description: Removes an agent. Rejects if referenced by channels or schedules.

patch.description: Mutates agent settings: tier, provider, model, cost limits, fallbacks, supervisor, etc.

get.description: Returns the content, editability, and agent-mutability of a persona section (identity, soul, user, or memory) for the specified agent.

parameters.description: Persona section name (identity, soul, user, memory)
parameters.name: section

put.description: Writes new content to a persona section (identity, soul, user, or memory) for the specified agent. The section must be editable.

parameters.description: Persona section name (identity, soul, user, memory)
parameters.name: section

get.description: Returns approval requests optionally filtered by status (pending, approved, denied, expired).

parameters.description: Filter by status: pending, approved, denied, expired
parameters.name: status

get.description: Returns a single approval request by ID.

post.description: Resolves a pending approval request. Used for both /approve and /deny endpoints. Optionally creates an auto-approve rule when auto_approve query param is set.
post.summary: Approve or deny an approval request

post.description: Resolves a pending approval request. Used for both /approve and /deny endpoints. Optionally creates an auto-approve rule when auto_approve query param is set.
post.summary: Approve or deny an approval request

get.description: Returns a paginated list of audit log events with optional filtering by category, agent, status, source, time range, and free-text search.

parameters.description: Filter by event category (e.g. tool_call, skill, channel, approval)
parameters.name: category

parameters.description: Filter by status (ok, error, pending, denied)
parameters.name: status

parameters.description: Maximum number of events to return
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Number of events to skip for pagination
parameters.name: offset

400.description: Invalid query parameter (since, until, limit, or offset)

get.description: Returns aggregate counts of audit events grouped by category and status, plus events in the last hour. Optionally filtered by a start time.

parameters.description: Only count events after this time (RFC3339 format)
parameters.name: since

get.description: Return which authentication methods (password, OIDC) are enabled and the preferred login method.

post.description: Authenticate with a bcrypt-hashed password and create a session cookie.
Clear the session cookie and end the current session.

get.description: Perform a fresh OIDC discovery against the configured issuer and return endpoint details.

post.description: Verify the current password and replace it with a new one (minimum 8 characters).
Update the preferred login method (auto, password, or apikey) and persist to config.

get.description: Verify the current session cookie and return authentication status.
Return all active sessions for the authenticated user, including the current session ID. API-key callers (no session cookie) receive an empty list.

delete.description: Revoke all active sessions for the authenticated user. API-key callers (no session cookie) receive {"revoked": 0}.
Revoke a single active session by its ID.

get.description: Return a detailed summary of auth configuration including password, OIDC, session, and API key state.
Returns all auto-approve rules, optionally filtered by agent name.

post.description: Creates a new auto-approve rule for a specific agent and tool. Scope can be 'session' (requires conversation_id) or 'permanent'.

parameters.description: Rule definition: agent, tool, scope, conversation_id
parameters.name: body

delete.description: Removes an auto-approve rule by ID.

get.description: Returns the current browser automation configuration.
Returns all stored browser automation profiles.
Returns detailed information about a specific browser profile.

delete.description: Removes a browser automation profile by name.

get.description: Returns active browser automation sessions.
Returns all configured channels with their agent bindings, adapter associations, and active adapter keys.

post.description: Creates a named channel bound to an agent with optional adapter bindings, delivery mode, and session mode. Persists the channel to the TOML config file.

400.description: Validation error (missing name/agent, invalid delivery or session_mode)

409.description: Channel already exists or channels not configured

get.description: Returns a single channel's configuration including agent binding, adapters, delivery/session mode, conversation ID, and active adapter keys.

delete.description: Removes a channel from the dispatcher and the TOML config file. Implicit channels cannot be deleted.

patch.description: Partially updates a channel's configuration (agent, adapters, delivery, session_mode). Only provided fields are applied; omitted fields retain their current values. Implicit channels cannot be modified. Persists changes to the TOML config file.

parameters.description: Fields to update (all optional except at least one)
parameters.name: body

400.description: Invalid JSON, invalid delivery/session_mode, or channel is implicit

404.description: Channel or referenced agent not found

post.description: Sets the active channel for a given adapter key, routing that adapter's messages through the specified channel.
post.summary: Activate a channel for an adapter key

200.description: Activation confirmation with channel and adapter_key

404.description: Channel not found or channels not configured

delete.description: Clears the active channel override for a given adapter key. Returns 409 if the adapter key is not currently active on this channel.
delete.summary: Deactivate a channel for an adapter key

409.description: Adapter key is not active on this channel

post.description: Sends a message to an agent. Returns JSON or SSE stream depending on Accept header.

get.description: Returns cost tracking data with per-agent and per-session breakdown
Returns server health status
Returns all API keys (without the secret token). Includes revoked keys.

post.description: Creates a new API key with the specified name and scopes. The plaintext key is returned only once.

delete.description: Soft-deletes an API key, marking it as revoked. The key can no longer be used for authentication.
Permanently removes an API key record from the database.

post.description: Generates a new secret for an existing API key. The new plaintext key is returned only once.

200.description: Rotated key with new plaintext token

get.description: Returns all key-value entries for the specified agent, optionally filtered by a key prefix.
get.summary: List KV entries for an agent

200.description: Object with entries array containing key, value, created_at, updated_at, and optional expires_at

get.description: Returns the value of a single key for the specified agent.

200.description: Object with key and value fields

put.description: Creates or updates a key-value pair for the specified agent. Optionally accepts a TTL duration string (e.g. "1h", "30m").

200.description: Object with key and value fields

delete.description: Removes a single key-value pair for the specified agent.

patch.description: Updates default provider, model, and cost limits. Syncs limits to live CostTracker.

get.description: Returns all configured LLM providers with their settings

post.description: Creates a new named provider instance and persists to TOML

delete.description: Removes a provider instance. Rejects if referenced by agents or default_provider.

patch.description: Updates a provider instance's configuration (API key, base URL, etc.)

get.description: Returns a list of available LLM model identifiers from all configured providers.
Returns enriched LLM model metadata from all configured providers, including pricing information. Optionally filtered by provider.
Returns the onboarding checklist with five setup milestones (auth, agent, adapter, provider, skill) and whether the onboarding card should be displayed.

post.description: Persists onboarding_dismissed=true to the TOML config so the onboarding card is permanently hidden.

get.description: Returns the generated OpenAPI 2.0 (Swagger) specification
Returns whether the system is in panic mode and when panic was triggered.

post.description: Triggers an emergency stop — cancels all in-flight requests and pauses the scheduler.

get.description: Returns all registered plugins with their type, status, and capabilities.

post.description: Registers a new plugin (subprocess or docker) and persists its config to TOML.

get.description: Returns detailed information about a specific plugin.

delete.description: Unregisters a plugin and removes its config from TOML.

post.description: Clears the panic state and resumes the scheduler.

get.description: Returns all registered schedule entries with their configuration, last run time, and next run time.

post.description: Creates a scheduled job bound to a channel and optionally a skill. Validates the cron expression, channel reference, and agent/skill existence. Persists the schedule to the TOML config file.

201.description: Created schedule with name, schedule expression, and status

400.description: Validation error (missing fields, invalid cron, unknown skill)

delete.description: Unregisters a schedule from the scheduler and removes it from the TOML config file.

patch.description: Partially updates a schedule's configuration. Only provided fields are applied; omitted fields retain their current values. Re-registers the schedule with the scheduler and persists changes to the TOML config file.

200.description: Updated schedule with name, schedule expression, and status

400.description: Invalid JSON, invalid cron expression, or unknown skill

get.description: Returns the current server configuration including listen address, TLS, CORS, WebSocket settings, and build info.

patch.description: Partially updates server configuration (external_url, timezone). Changes are applied in-memory and persisted to the TOML config file.

post.description: Re-reads the TOML configuration file and applies changes. Returns an error if no reload function is configured or the reload fails.
Triggers a graceful server process restart. The response is sent before the restart occurs. Returns an error if no restart function is configured.

get.description: Returns all conversations from the memory store with pagination. When backed by a TelemetryStore, includes telemetry stats per session.

parameters.description: Maximum number of sessions to return (max 500)
parameters.name: limit

200.description: Paginated session list with total count

delete.description: Permanently deletes a conversation and all its messages.

post.description: Removes all messages from a session while keeping the conversation row for session identity.

parameters.description: Agent name hint for engine resolution
parameters.name: agent

post.description: Summarises the conversation via LLM and replaces all messages with a single summary message.

parameters.description: Agent name hint for engine resolution
parameters.name: agent

get.description: Returns the message history for a specific conversation, including per-message telemetry.
Returns skill usage records for a conversation.
Returns aggregated telemetry statistics for a conversation including token counts, costs, and model breakdown.

post.description: Cancels an in-flight LLM request for the given session.

get.description: Returns tool call records for a conversation including tool name, server, duration, and success/error status.
Returns whether initial setup is required (no active API keys or password exist) and whether PIN-based account setup is available. No authentication required.

post.description: Creates the first API key during initial setup. Returns 409 once any active key exists. No authentication required — the endpoint locks itself after first use.

parameters.description: Key name and scopes (defaults to admin if empty)
parameters.name: body

post.description: Creates an admin account (password login) verified by a one-time setup PIN displayed in server logs at startup. No authentication required — the endpoint self-disables after successful use.

get.description: Returns all skills across all agents, deduplicated by agent+name. Each entry includes the owning agent name.

200.description: List of skills with agent field

get.description: Returns all skills registered for the specified agent.

post.description: Creates a skill for the specified agent. Writes the skill file to the agent's persona directory and registers it in memory.

get.description: Returns a single skill's full definition including name, description, version, triggers, body, and owning agent.

put.description: Updates a skill's fields for the specified agent. If the name field differs from the path parameter, the skill is renamed (old file removed, new file created). Returns 409 if the new name conflicts with an existing skill.

parameters.description: Fields to update (all optional; omit to keep current value)
parameters.name: body

409.description: New name conflicts with existing skill

delete.description: Removes a skill from the specified agent. Deletes both the in-memory registration and the skill file on disk.

get.description: Returns aggregated telemetry across all sessions for A/B comparison, with optional time-range filtering.
Returns all registered MCP tool servers with their connection status.

post.description: Registers a new MCP tool server (stdio or SSE transport) and persists its config to TOML.

get.description: Handles the OAuth provider redirect after user authorization. Returns an HTML page indicating success or failure. This endpoint requires no authentication as it is a browser redirect from the external OAuth provider.

parameters.description: Authorization code from the OAuth provider
parameters.name: code

parameters.description: Error code if authorization was denied
parameters.name: error

get.description: Returns all active pending OAuth authorization flows across all tools.
Returns detailed information about a specific MCP tool server including config, status, and OAuth state.

put.description: Updates the configuration of an existing MCP tool server and reconnects it.

delete.description: Unregisters an MCP tool server and removes its config from TOML.

get.description: Returns the MCP tool definitions (name, description, parameters) for a specific tool server.
Returns the health status of a specific MCP tool server including uptime and restart count.
Returns the current OAuth token status for the specified tool, including whether a token exists, its expiry, scopes, and whether re-authorization is needed.
get.summary: Get OAuth token status for a tool

post.description: Starts an OAuth authorization flow for the specified tool. Triggers a tool reconnection that creates a pending authorization, then returns the auth URL for the UI to open in a browser popup. Polls up to 30 seconds for the pending auth to appear.
post.summary: Initiate OAuth connection for a tool

503.description: OAuth support not configured or lifecycle manager unavailable

504.description: Timed out waiting for OAuth flow to start

delete.description: Deletes the stored OAuth token for the specified tool, clearing both the handler's cached token and the persisted token store entry.
delete.summary: Revoke OAuth token for a tool

503.description: OAuth support not configured or lifecycle manager unavailable

post.description: Manually restarts an MCP tool server, reconnecting the subprocess or SSE connection.

adapter_name.description: AdapterName identifies which adapter to use for confirmation messages.

callback_data.description:
CallbackData is the base prefix embedded in Telegram inline button data.
Format: "appr:{id}" — buttons append ":approve" or ":deny".

conversation_id.description: ConversationID links this approval to the engine conversation that created it.

external_id.description: ExternalID is the adapter-level chat/channel ID to reply to after resolution.

payload.description: Payload is the content to apply when approved (e.g. full USER.md text).

resolved_by.description: ResolvedBy records who resolved the approval: "telegram", "api", "ws", "expired", or "auto_approve".

summary.description: Summary is a human-readable one-liner shown in the approval UI.

model.description: required for switch_model; optional for switch_provider

threshold.description: Deprecated: legacy low_funds field, auto-migrated on load.

effort.description:
Effort sets the reasoning effort level: "xhigh", "high", "medium",
"low", "minimal", "none". Mutually exclusive with MaxTokens.

enabled.description:
Enabled activates reasoning with model defaults. Inferred true when
Effort or MaxTokens is set.

exclude.description: Exclude omits reasoning from the response (tokens are still billed).

max_tokens.description: MaxTokens sets the reasoning token budget. Mutually exclusive with Effort.
